Dr Jack Kruse is a practicing neurosurgeon, biophysicist and health optimisation researcher. He has done extensive research into the causes of human disease with a highly unique perspective grounded in bio-physics.

This is an excerpt of Part II of the Jack Kruse Series. In these conversations we explore Dr Kruse’s paradigm of health centered on Light, Water and Magnetism, mitochondrial and circadian function and how these are impacted to influence human health and disease.

snippet.title: US Marines Experience In Ukraine's Right Sector - TELL ALL - Foreign Fighter Interview
snippet.description:
G'day Legends, I hope you're having a good day, Gibby a former US Marine deployed into Ukraine firstly to train but then later on to fight alongside other foreigners and the Ukrainian Military. 

If you're new here thanks for coming across, I served in the Australian Infantry from 2014-2021, With a tour to Afghanistan as a crew commander of a Armoured Mobility Vehicle. 
Upon my Return I was unexpectedly diagnosed with a Incurable and Inoperable Brain tumour that is slowly killing me. I was also awarded the Queens Order of Australia Medal (OAM) hence the post nominals after my name. Then Being medically separated from the Army I flew to Ukraine in 2022 for 6.5months and now make content full time. I really appreciate you being here Thankyou

snippet.title: Outlive by Peter Attia with Bill Gifford | Book Summary
snippet.description:
Welcome to the book summary Outlive - The Science and Art of Longevity by Peter Attia. In this book summary, you'll learn that Peter Attia's transformative book, Outlive, serves as an all-encompassing manual, providing invaluable insights into extending lifespan, promoting well-being, and achieving a more gratifying existence. By integrating the latest scientific advancements with practical advice, this enlightening guide empowers individuals to optimize their exercise routines, dietary choices, sleep patterns, and emotional equilibrium, thereby maximizing their longevity.

Peter Attia, a distinguished physician and esteemed expert in the field of longevity, centers his work on the science of human optimization. With an MD from Stanford University, he has cultivated an illustrious career that includes notable positions at renowned institutions such as The Johns Hopkins Hospital and the National Cancer Institute. Attia's influence extends beyond the written word, as he captivates audiences through his immensely popular podcast, The Peter Attia Drive, where he delves into the realms of nutrition, exercise, and mental well-being.

In collaboration with Bill Gifford, a highly respected authority on health and wellness, blending his talents in both journalism and science, Outlive represents the culmination of their expertise. Gifford, acclaimed for his best-selling works including Spring Chicken and Ledyard, adds an additional layer of wisdom to this exceptional collaboration.

The modern era has ushered in a myriad of deadly diseases, including cancer, heart disease, and type 2 diabetes, among others. Despite the advancements in our lifestyles compared to our ancestors, certain aspects of our contemporary world are adversely affecting our health.

The central predicament lies in the profound changes our environment has undergone in recent centuries, while our genetic makeup remains unchanged. Take fructose, for instance. Once a benign substance found in fruits and honey, it has now permeated our food supply, leading to excessive fat storage. However, the detriments to our well-being extend beyond our dietary choices. Factors such as sedentary lifestyles, disrupted sleep patterns, and the impact of social media on our emotional state exert a significant toll. Consequently, it becomes imperative to devise a comprehensive strategy to thrive in this unconventional new world.

Outlive focuses its attention on the four key areas that warrant concentration for enhancing health: exercise, nutrition, sleep, and emotional well-being. Rejecting the notion of a one-size-fits-all approach, the book provides readers with the tools to construct a personalized framework, tailored to their unique needs. It emphasizes the importance of remaining receptive to experimentation and adaptability, eschewing rigid adherence to specific diets or exercise ideologies. The ultimate objective is to foster longevity and well-being, continuously surpassing the accomplishments of the previous decades, thereby enriching each phase of life.

Outlive is not a mere book; it represents a transformative expedition that revolutionizes our perception and cultivation of personal well-being. With Attia and Gifford as trusted guides, readers embark on an extraordinary quest to unlock their full potential, transcending the constraints of time, and embracing a life characterized by vitality and purpose.

Book link: https://amzn.to/420uGEr

snippet.title: "Outlive: Mastering the Science and Art of Living Longer"
snippet.description:
In "Outlive: The Science and Art of Longevity," acclaimed journalist Bill Gifford explores the latest research on aging and longevity. Drawing on interviews with leading scientists and experts, Gifford provides a fascinating and accessible overview of the science of aging, from the cellular level to the societal level. But he doesn't stop there - Gifford also delves into the art of living a longer, healthier life, with practical tips and insights on everything from diet and exercise to stress management and social connections. Whether you're in your 20s or your 80s, "Outlive" is an essential guide to living a longer, healthier, and more fulfilling life.

#longevity
#healthylifestyle
 #aging

snippet.title: 9/11/01 Live Regis and Kelly (Entire 58 minutes of the show)
snippet.description:
I recorded the syndicated Live with Regis and Kelly show every morning on my VCR.  I was able to receive the live feed that was being transmitted by satellite to affiliate TV stations across the country at 9am EDT.  I received the signal from my C-band satellite system in Iowa at 8am CDT and could watch the show live.  I had never shared the VHS video with anyone until I posted a 5 minute segment of the shows opening  in a private Facebook group after Regis Philbin had died in July of 2020.   I hadn't planned to share the entire video until I was told recently that the 5 minute segment had been posted on YouTube by Jake Allonar.  He came across the video clip of a Facebook link.
Since there have been over 3 million views of the 5 minute clip and many of the comments wondered what followed after the commercial break on the show, I am sharing the entire show.  Present day technology uses encrypted digital transmission of syndicated shows instead of unencrypted satellite transmission as was available in 2001.
This material may be protected by copyright law (Title 17 U.S. Code)

snippet.title: 248 ‒ OUTLIVE book: A behind-the-scenes look into the writing of this book, motivation & main themes
snippet.description:
Order OUTLIVE: https://peterattiamd.com/outlive/

Watch the full episode and view show notes here: https://bit.ly/3Z4K94Q
Become a member to receive exclusive content: https://peterattiamd.com/subscribe/
Sign up to receive Peter's email newsletter: https://peterattiamd.com/newsletter/

After more than six years of research, planning, and writing, Peter's book, Outlive: The Science and Art of Longevity, is officially available as of March 28th. In this episode, Peter sits down with co-author Bill Gifford to provide a behind-the-scenes look into the writing process, including the motivation for making it happen, how the book evolved over the course of the writing process, and why certain topics were chosen (and omitted). Additionally, they discuss how the book is structured and touch on a few of the book's main themes to give potential readers an idea of what they can expect.

We discuss:
0:00:00 - Intro 
0:00:08 - The meaning of the book's title and subtitle
0:07:08 - Finding the right art for the book cover
0:15:47 - Who is Bill Gifford, and how did he get involved in the book?
0:26:47 - How Peter's writing evolved over the six years it took to write this book
0:31:44 - The structure of the book and what people can expect to learn
0:36:38 - How the writing of the book and the podcast interviews have shaped Peter's thinking and approach to translating science
0:40:35 - Making the book stand the test of time despite the constant evolution of science and medicine
0:44:09 - Objective, strategy, and tactics
0:48:00 - Exciting possible progressions in science and medicine over the next decade
0:52:43 - What is holding back medicine 3.0 from being the norm?
0:56:04 - How the book compares to the podcast in terms of technicality and readability for the layman
1:06:36 - Motivation to write the book and insights into challenges around the writing process
1:22:56 - Peter's decision to be the reader for the audiobook
1:33:05 - The many painstaking last-minute changes and edits that brought the book together and made it better
1:43:59 - Peter and Bill's favorite parts of the book
1:49:15 - The incredible team of people supporting the book

--------
About:

The Peter Attia Drive is a weekly, ultra-deep-dive podcast focusing on maximizing health, longevity, critical thinking…and a few other things. With over 45 million episodes downloaded, it features topics including fasting, ketosis, Alzheimer’s disease, cancer, mental health, and much more.

Peter is a physician focusing on the applied science of longevity. His practice deals extensively with nutritional interventions, exercise physiology, sleep physiology, emotional and mental health, and pharmacology to increase lifespan (delay the onset of chronic disease), while simultaneously improving healthspan (quality of life).

Learn more: https://peterattiamd.com

snippet.title: Genes Matter In Extreme Longevity: CONCENTRATE ON HEALTHSPAN (Peter Attia, MD)
snippet.description:
Watch the entire video here: https://youtu.be/u378odYz0io

In this video, Russ Roberts interviews Peter Attia, MD, about healthspan and lifespan. Mr. Attia clearly explains that genes are everything when living to an extreme old age. He emphasized that we must concentrate on healthspan more than anything.

Who Is Peter Attia?

Peter Attia, MD, is a prominent figure in the field of medicine and health. He is a medical doctor who received his medical degree from the Stanford University School of Medicine and underwent training at the Johns Hopkins Hospital. He is known for his work in areas such as longevity, health optimization, and metabolic health.

Dr. Peter Attia is the founder of Early Medical and is also involved in podcasting. He hosts "The Peter Attia Drive," a podcast where he explores various topics related to health, science, and medicine. His educational background includes a B.Sc. in mechanical engineering and applied mathematics.

Additionally, Dr. Attia has authored works such as "DR. PETER ATTIA BIOGRAPHY: His Science and Art," which delves into his scientific and medical insights. And more recently, "Outlive."

Outlive is a book written by Peter Attia, MD, with Bill Gifford, that compiles the latest science on health and longevity, combined with practical advice anyone can use to live better today and beat four types of chronic disease with the four pillars of health: exercise, nutrition, sleep, and emotional health.

The book aims to empower readers to optimize their lifestyle for maximum longevity, drawing on cutting-edge science and practical advice.

snippet.title: Ambassador Kennedy and Son Jack Recreate JFK's PT-109 Swim
snippet.description:
Eighty years ago, President Kennedy swam between Plum Pudding Island (now Kennedy Island), Naru Island, and Olasana Island — multiple times, at night — to save himself and his PT-109 crew. 

While in Solomon Islands, Ambassador Kennedy and her son, Jack, recreated the swim to commemorate the heroism and perseverance of the President and his crew.
